_meta.label: T4.1: cold-vs-warm same-prompt-twice + explicit cache_control
_meta.user_question: Briefly explain why gamma is highest at-the-money for an option, in one paragraph.

results.label: C1-cold
results.visible_first_300: Gamma peaks at-the-money because it measures delta's sensitivity to underlying price changes, and delta (≈ N(d₁)) transitions most rapidly around S ≈ K. Mathematically, gamma ∝ N'(d₁)/(S·σ·√T), where the standard normal PDF N'(·) is maximized at d₁ = 0—exactly the ATM condition. Intuition: an ATM op

results.label: C2-warm
results.visible_first_300: Gamma peaks at-the-money because it measures the rate of change of delta, and delta undergoes its steepest transition near the strike price. For a European option, gamma = φ(d₁)/(S·σ·√T) where φ is the standard normal PDF—mathematically maximized when d₁ = 0, i.e., S = K. Deep ITM or OTM, delta is a

results.label: C3-explicit-cache_control
results.visible_first_300: Gamma measures the rate of change of delta with respect to the underlying price: Γ = ∂Δ/∂S = ∂²V/∂S².
